nodes.context: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Western Canada, founded in Red Deer, Alberta in 1968.
Outlaw motorcycle club, dominant in Red Deer and Calgary, rival of Rebels.
One of the four dominant outlaw motorcycle clubs in Alberta prior to 1997.
Outlaw motorcycle club, tolerated in Calgary, ally of Rebels.
Outlaw motorcycle club that patched over Grim Reapers and later Rebels chapters.
Motorcycle club from Saskatchewan that merged with Rebels in the 1980s.
Outlaw motorcycle club that Rebels attempted to patch over to in 2001.
Outlaw motorcycle club listed as an ally of Rebels in infobox.
Motorcycle club listed as an ally of Rebels in infobox.
Motorcycle club listed as a rival of Rebels in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1970s the Rebels had multiple violent exchanges with their main rivals in the province- the Grim Reapers Motorcycle Club.
edges.context: Rebels and Grim Reapers were rivals with violent clashes in the 1970s.

edges.evidence_quote: after merging with the Loners Motorcycle Club of Saskatchewan in the 1980s
edges.context: Rebels merged with Loners Motorcycle Club in the 1980s.

edges.evidence_quote: when the Grim Reapers became part of the Hells Angels in a patch-over ceremony held in Red Deer, Alberta
edges.context: Grim Reapers were patched over into Hells Angels in 1997.

edges.evidence_quote: In September 1998, the Saskatoon Rebels were patched over to the Hells Angels
edges.context: Rebels chapters patched over to Hells Angels in 1998.

edges.evidence_quote: Joey 'Crazy Horse' Morin, Vice President acting on behalf of Pete Sorenson (president) for the Edmonton chapter of the Rebels Motorcycle Club, first contacted the Bandidos with the aim of 'patching over'
edges.context: Rebels attempted to patch over to Bandidos in 2001.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels
edges.context: Kings Crew listed as an ally of Rebels in infobox.
